title: practical english usage - Part 325

For here's and there's followed by plural nouns, ►     130.6.

There are similar differences between this and that (►  144), come and go (►  424) and bring and take(* 409).

or                                                    but not Did you like that place?   Did you like it there? (          Did you like there?) but not This place is terrible. It is terrible here, (          Here is terrible.) Note that here and there cannot normally be used as nouns.

vocabulary' •  489  home Ring up and see if Jacqueline is at the Smiths', could you?

We had a great evening at Philip's.

hos, etc). One way of saying this is to use at with a possessive.

somebody’ s home (like French chez, German bei, Danish/Swedish/Norwegian There is no special preposition in English to express the idea of being at not I think I'll go home, (     .. . -to home.) Home (without to) can be used as an adverb referring to direction.

At is often dropped, especially in American English.

Is anybody at home? (     .. .-at-the-home?) No article is used in the expression at home (meaning 'in one’ s own place’ ).

1  articles      and   prepositions 489 home       _____________ as Thanksgiving) when people do not have to work often used in American English for a day of publicly observed celebration (such mainly used for the periods when universities are not teaching.) Holiday is most Americans more often use the word vacation. (In British English, vacation is not I met Marianne on holiday in Norway, (     .. . on/in holidays-...) The  singular is used in the British expression on holiday (note the preposition).

Next Monday is a public holiday.

We get five days' Christmas holiday this year.

Where are you going for your summer  holiday(s)?

year. In other cases we  normally use the singular holiday. Compare: In British English, the plural holidays is often used for the 'long holiday’  of the 488  holiday        and   holi    days            _  _  _  ____ Was King Arthur a historical figure?

historical research a historical novel historical documents in history’ .

Historical means 'connected with the study of history' or 'really existing Our  two countries are about to make a historic agreement.

We spent the summer  visiting historic houses and castles in France.

customs, etc, and for moments which  'make history'.

Historic is used especially for historically important places, remains, 487 historic        and   historical We let the upstairs room to a student.

rooms, houses, etc.

Let is used in British English, like rent (out), to talk about selling the use of 2 let Word  Problems from A  to Z     Section 31 vocabulary' •  490  hope How did you do it? Tell me how you did it. I know how he did it.

How is used to introduce questions or the answers to questions.

1 use   and   word    order 492 how Hopefully I'm not disturbing you?

Hopefully, inflation will soon be under control.

Another meaning is 'it is to be hoped that’  or 'I hope’ .

She sat there waiting hopefully for the phone to ring.

One meaning of hopefully is 'full of hope’ , 'hoping’ .

491 hopefully             _________ ►    457 hope,     expect,       wait     and   look     forward For the differences between hope, expect, wait and look forward, ►    457.

For more about the use of past censes in polite requests, ►    311.    For I hope so/not, ►    585.

I had hoped that Jennifer would study medicine, but she didn't want to.

I had hoped... refers to hopes for things that did not happen.

I was hoping you could lend me some money.

We   can use I was hoping... to introduce a polite request.

3 special     uses    of  past    tenses For negative structures with think, believe, etc, >219.

I hope she doesn't wake up. (     I don't hope she wakes up.) In negative sentences, we  usually put not with the verb that comes after hope.

2 negative       sentences For a similar use of present tenses after bet, ►    401.

I hope she likes (= will like) the flowers.  I hope the bus comes soon.
